      "D": "Metaphor"

    "explanation": "Unlike fables, parables usually involve human characters and realistic situations to teach an ethical or spiritual truth."

      "B": "Blank verse",
      "C": "Free verse",
      "D": "Lyric poetry"
    },
    "answer": "A",
    "explanation": "Concrete poetry relies on typographical layout to convey its message, where the visual form is just as important as the text."

    "options": {
      "A": "Deus ex machina",
      "B": "In medias res",
      "C": "Catharsis",
      "D": "Hamartia"
    },
    "answer": "A",
    "explanation": "Deus ex machina (Latin for 'god from the machine') occurs when a seemingly unsolvable problem is suddenly and abruptly resolved by an unexpected intervention."

  {
    "subject": "Literature",
    "topic": "Figures of Speech",
    "year": 2011,
    "question": "Identify the figure of speech in this statement: 'The pen is mightier than the sword.'",
    "options": {
      "A": "Synecdoche",
      "B": "Metonymy",
      "C": "Metaphor",
      "D": "Personification"
    },
    "answer": "B",
    "explanation": "Metonymy is used here because 'pen' stands for written words/diplomacy, and 'sword' stands for military force/violence—items closely associated with the concepts they represent."
  },
  {
    "subject": "Literature",
    "topic": "Figures of Speech",
    "year": 2011,
    "question": "'All hands must be on deck to solve this problem.' The use of 'hands' to represent people is an example of ______.",
    "options": {
      "A": "Synecdoche",
      "B": "Metonymy",
      "C": "Hyperbole",
      "D": "Simile"
    },
    "answer": "A",
    "explanation": "Synecdoche is a figure of speech where a part of something (hands) is used to represent the whole (the workers/people)."
  },
  {
    "subject": "Literature",
    "topic": "Figures of Speech",
    "year": 2012,
    "question": "The statement 'He passed away peacefully in his sleep' instead of 'He died' is an example of ______.",
    "options": {
      "A": "Paradox",
      "B": "Euphemism",
      "C": "Oxymoron",
      "D": "Litotes"
    },
    "answer": "B",
    "explanation": "Euphemism is the substitution of a mild, indirect, or vague expression for one thought to be offensive, harsh, or blunt."

  {
    "subject": "Literature",

    "options": {

      "B": "Theme",
      "C": "Conflict",
      "D": "Setting"
    },
    "answer": "B",
    "explanation": "The theme is the underlying meaning or universal truth explored in the play, distinct from the plot, which is just the sequence of events."

    "options": {

    "year": 2010,
    "question": "A complex French verse form, usually unrhymed, consisting of six stanzas of six lines each and a three-line envoy, is a ______.",
    "options": {
      "A": "Villanelle",
      "B": "Sestina",
      "C": "Sonnet",
      "D": "Ode"
    },
    "answer": "B",

      "D": "Epistolary narrative"
    },
    "answer": "A",
    "explanation": "Stream of consciousness ignores traditional syntax and logic to mimic how the human brain actually processes thoughts in real-time."
